_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 statewide executives (AL/AR/CA). Currency fixes: wes-allen LOST 2026 AL Lt.Gov GOP runoff (6/16/26) to Wahl, remains sitting SoS thru Jan 2027; will-ainsworth term-limited as LG + declined 2026 governor race, exiting Jan 2027; eleni-kounalakis withdrew from governor (8/8/25) -> now running CA State Treasurer 2026, term-limited LG; leslie-rutledge running LG reelection (effectively unopposed); rob-bonta running AG reelection (declined governor).

profile.religion: Southern Baptist — First Baptist Church of Troy (ordained deacon)
profile.background: AL Secretary of State since 2023; former AL House (HD89) 2018-2022 and Pike County Probate Judge. Ran for Lt. Governor in 2026 and lost the GOP runoff to John Wahl on 6/16/2026; remains sitting SoS through Jan 2027.

wes-allen.notes_set: 2026 currency: gave up SoS to run for AL Lt. Governor; forced to runoff (Wahl 40.6% / Allen 38.0%, 5/19/26), then LOST runoff to John Wahl ~57-43 on 6/16/2026. Still sitting Secretary of State through Jan 2027. Strong documented Christian-conservative record as SoS and former state rep.

0.note: "Unapologetically pro-life... legal protection from conception until natural death"; co-sponsored 2019 Human Life Protection Act; AL Citizens for Life cited 100% pro-life record.

1.note: Co-sponsored 2019 Human Life Protection Act, a near-total (abolition-level) abortion ban.

3.note: Affirms legal protection 'until natural death' — opposes euthanasia/quality-of-life rationing.

4.note: Endorsed by AL Citizens for Life; no evidence of abortion-industry PAC funding for a consistently pro-life Republican.

0.note: "Marriage is a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman."

1.note: Affirms exclusive one-man-one-woman definition of marriage in law.

2.note: Primary House sponsor of the Vulnerable Child Compassion and Protection Act (VCAP/SB184) banning minors' gender-transition procedures; opposes biological males in women's sports.

4.note: Sponsored VCAP and opposes males in women's sports — opposes LGBTQ-ideology promotion affecting children.

1.note: VCAP bans gender-transition medical procedures for minors — protects minors from gender interventions.

0.note: Self-described born-again evangelical, ordained deacon; opposes government restriction on religious practice.

1.note: Supports Masterpiece Cakeshop — conscience exemptions for Christian business owners.
Partnered with DHS-USCIS SAVE to verify citizenship of registrants; AL has photo voter ID.

3.note: Champion of SB1 (2024) ballot-harvesting ban (Class B felony for paid third-party absentee handling).

2.note: Supports eliminating the federal Department of Education ('Strongly Agree') — resists federal overreach on education.

4.note: Withdrew AL from ERIC and built state-controlled AVID database — opposed external/federal capture of state election data.

profile.religion: Baptist — Gilliam Springs Baptist Church, Arab, AL (former youth pastor)
profile.background: AL Lt. Governor since 2019; former state rep (2014-2018); businessman. Term-limited as LG and announced 5/21/2025 he would NOT run for governor in 2026, plans to exit public office Jan 2027.

will-ainsworth.notes_set: 2026 currency: term-limited as Lt. Governor (no third consecutive term) and declined the 2026 governor's race ('I will not be a candidate for governor', 5/21/2025); exiting public office Jan 2027 — NOT seeking any office in 2026. Record is thin on explicit personal positions because many AL conservative wins of his era are formally Gov. Ivey's signature actions with Ainsworth as advocate; only Ainsworth-specific HARD evidence scored.

0.note: "Abortion is murder. Those three simple words sum up my position."

1.note: Backed the 2019 near-total ban with 'no amendments' — opposed rape/incest exceptions (abolition-level).

4.note: Consistent pro-life ('abortion is murder'); no evidence of abortion-industry PAC funding.

0.note: Championed the CHOOSE Act (universal ESA); 'I support school choice 100%.'

2.note: Promoted banning critical race theory in schools.

0.note: Vocal champion of AL constitutional/permitless carry (HB272, 2022).

profile.background: AR Lt. Governor since 2023 (first woman in the office); former AR Attorney General 2015-2023. Running for LG reelection in 2026 (no GOP/Dem primary filed against her; Libertarian on general ballot). Deep AG record of multistate litigation.

leslie-rutledge.notes_set: 2026 currency: confirmed running for Lt. Governor reelection (only GOP filer; no Dem filed by 11/12/2025; Libertarian Kalagias on Nov 2026 general ballot). Rich AG-era (2015-2023) HARD record drives the scores. Religion/church affiliation: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. Two prompt assumptions NOT confirmed (PP-defunding defense; specific Dobbs amicus authorship) — left null.

0.note: As AG certified AR's Human Life Protection Act trigger ban (near-total; life-of-mother only).

1.note: Certified a near-total ban with no rape/incest exception (abolition-level).

4.note: Consistent pro-life AG record; no evidence of abortion-industry PAC funding.

0.note: As AG defended AR's same-sex marriage ban.

1.note: Defended marriage ban; challenged Fayetteville anti-discrimination ordinance; delayed Pavan birth-certificate implementation.

2.note: Defended the SAFE Act banning gender-transition procedures for minors.

4.note: Challenged the Fayetteville LGBTQ ordinance and defended the SAFE Act.

0.note: Advocated school choice in the 2022 Lt. Gov debate.

1.note: Defended the SAFE Act protecting minors from gender-transition procedures.

2.note: Joined 22-AG suit vs. Biden USDA SOGI gender-identity guidance tied to school-lunch funds.

3.note: As AG prepared to defend the Ten Commandments Monument (Act 1231) at the AR Capitol.
Joined multistate amicus in PA (Boockvar) opposing late/no-postmark mail ballots.

0.note: Sued over the ARPA tax-cut 'mandate' (anti-commandeering / 10th-Amendment claim).

1.note: Joined 21-state suit vs. the federal travel mask mandate.

2.note: Sued over Title IX gender reinterpretation (federal overreach on education/women's sports).

3.note: Repeatedly asserted state sovereignty against unconstitutional federal action (ARPA, student loans, Title IX).

1.note: Joined Texas-led suit to end DACA — aligned with deporting those who entered as minors.

4.note: Led 16-state amicus defending the NRA vs. NY AG's dissolution suit — opposed a citizen-disarmament-adjacent action.

profile.religion: Greek Orthodox (St. Paul Medal, Greek Orthodox Archdiocese of America)
profile.background: CA Lt. Governor since 2019 (first woman elected to the office); former U.S. Ambassador to Hungary. Term-limited as LG. Withdrew from the 2026 governor's race on 8/8/2025 and is now running for CALIFORNIA STATE TREASURER (2026).

eleni-kounalakis.notes_set: 2026 currency: launched a gubernatorial bid in 2023 then WITHDREW 8/8/2025 and switched to running for CA State Treasurer; term-limited as LG. Standard progressive profile (pro-choice w/ PP+NARAL+EMILY's List endorsements, pro-LGBTQ/created first statewide transgender council, pro-gun-control, pro-sanctuary, pro mail-in voting). DEVIATION: documented CSLEA law-enforcement union endorsement (pro-police data point for a Democrat).

0.note: Pro-choice; defends abortion access ('California refuses to back down from protecting women's freedom').

1.note: Backed CA Prop 1 enshrining abortion in the constitution — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List, Planned Parenthood, and NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All (abortion-industry PAC alignment).

0.note: Opposed Prop 8; pro-LGBTQ; Equality California endorsement.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Created the nation's first statewide transgender advisory council (2021).

4.note: Equality California endorsement; statewide LGBTQ promotion.

3.note: Supports/expands universal mail-in voting — opposite of opposing mass mail-in.

0.note: DEVIATION: endorsed by the California Statewide Law Enforcement Association (CSLEA) — a documented pro-police data point for a Democrat.

1.note: Opposed federal immigration enforcement (ICE raids, census exclusion) — opposite of mandatory deportation.

2.note: Backs CA sanctuary posture; defended immigrants against federal enforcement.

1.note: Pledged to 'expand and strengthen background checks'; publicly refused the NRA questionnaire (#everytown) — supports gun control.

profile.background: CA Attorney General since 2021 (first Filipino-American AG); former CA Assemblymember 2012-2021. Declined the 2026 governor's race (Feb 2025) to seek a full term as AG. Has led 50+ multistate lawsuits against the Trump administration.

rob-bonta.notes_set: 2026 currency: confirmed running for AG REELECTION 2026 (declined the governor's race, early Feb 2025; on June 2026 primary ballot vs. Michael Gates (R)). Consistently progressive across every category with HARD evidence (PP-defunding-block litigation, trans-care defense, AB 1955 enforcement, ESG/climate litigation, anti-voter-ID/SAVE-Act, cash-bail abolition, sanctuary/birthright defense, gun-control defense). Factual correction: do NOT attribute AB 392 use-of-force to Bonta (Weber/McCarty). Religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND.

0.note: Led 22-state coalition blocking Planned Parenthood defunding; pro-choice.

1.note: Defends abortion access — opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Affiliates of CA and NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All.

0.note: Cites the 'freedom to marry'; Equality California endorsement.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Sued to block restrictions on gender-affirming care for minors; defends CA trans care.

4.note: Equality California endorsement; defends LGBTQ policy.

1.note: Enforces AB 1955 barring school parental-notification on gender; sued Chino Valley Unified; opposes parental-rights initiative.

4.note: Pursues ESG/climate-deception litigation against oil companies — promotes ESG agenda.

1.note: Joined 12-AG coalition urging rejection of the SAVE Act (citizenship-proof voter registration); sued to strike Shasta County voter-ID measure.

3.note: Sued to block a federal mail-in-voting restriction EO — defends mail-in voting.

1.note: Authored AB 42 to END cash bail — supports cashless bail (contrary to the rubric position).
Defends sanctuary law and sues to block deportations — opposite of mandatory deportation.

2.note: Defends SB 54 (CA Values Act sanctuary law).

4.note: Sued to block the birthright-citizenship EO — defends birthright citizenship.

1.note: Defends and appeals to uphold CA assault-weapons and large-capacity-magazine bans — supports gun control.
